description: Read any Medium author, publication, or tag as a local, full-text-searchable corpus — sourced directly from Medium's own public surfaces (RSS feeds, article pages, and Medium's internal GraphQL endpoint), with no API key and no third-party proxy.

tools.name: analytics
tools.description: Analyze locally synced data with count, group-by, and summary operations. Data must be synced first with the sync command.

params.name: db
params.description: Database path

params.name: group-by
params.description: Field to group by

params.name: limit
params.description: Max groups to show (default: 25)

params.name: type
params.description: Resource type to analyze

tools.name: author_archive
tools.description: Mirror a Medium writer's articles into the local SQLite store for offline, full-text search. Sourced directly from Medium's internal GraphQL endpoint (the article id list) and the article-page surface (each body), with no API key and no cookies. The argument is the writer's Medium user id (the stable hex id, e.g. bcab753a4d4e) OR their @handle / username (e.g. @quincylarson or quincylarson). A handle is resolved to its user id keylessly, by reading the author's public profile page — no API key, no cookies. You can also find a user id from a feed result's author_id field.

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

params.name: db
params.description: Path to the local SQLite store (default: standard data dir)

params.name: max-articles
params.description: Maximum number of articles to archive (default: 25)

tools.name: author_compare
tools.description: Compare two writers on output cadence, topic mix, and engagement (claps and voters per article).

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

params.name: db
params.description: Path to the local SQLite store (default: standard data dir)

tools.name: context
tools.description: Get API domain context: resource taxonomy, auth requirements, query tips, and unique capabilities. Call this first.

tools.name: corpus
tools.description: Full-text and regex search across everything you have synced locally (authors, publications, tags).

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

params.name: db
params.description: Path to the local SQLite store (default: standard data dir)

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 20)

params.name: regex
params.description: Treat the query as a Go regular expression (default: false)

tools.name: digest
tools.description: A deduped, ranked 'what is new since last sync' across the authors, publications, and tags you have archived.

params.name: db
params.description: Path to the local SQLite store (default: standard data dir)

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 30)

params.name: since
params.description: Only include articles published within this window (e.g. 7d, 2w, 36h) (default: 7d)

tools.name: feed
tools.description: Read a Medium RSS feed for an author, publication, or tag, sourced directly from medium.com with no API key and no cookies. Reference auto-detection: @<handle> an author -> medium.com/feed/@<handle> tag/<tag> a topic tag -> medium.com/feed/tag/<tag> <slug> a publication -> medium.com/feed/<slug> To read a tag, prefix it with "tag/" (a bare token is treated as a publication slug). To read an author, prefix the handle with "@".

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Fetch a single Medium article and render its body as Markdown, sourced directly from the article page with no API key and no cookies. Accepts either a full Medium URL or a bare article id: read 818e7841df9c read https://medium.com/p/818e7841df9c read https://author.medium.com/some-title-818e7841df9c Locked (member-only) posts return Medium's truncated preview when read anonymously; the output is flagged as a preview. Supplying your own Medium session cookie unlocks the full body (see 'auth' once Tier-1 auth is wired).

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search Medium for posts matching a free-text query, sourced directly from Medium's own internal GraphQL endpoint with no API key and no cookies. Results are paginated up to --limit (default 10). Each result is a normalized post summary (id, title, author, url, published date). Because search uses Medium's unversioned internal API, a search-surface outage returns a clear "surface unavailable" message; the feed and read commands, which do not depend on it, keep working.

params.name: args
params.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10)

tools.name: sql
tools.description: Run read-only SQL against local database. Use for ad-hoc analysis, aggregations, and joins across synced resources. Requires sync first.

params.name: query
params.description: SQL query (SELECT or WITH...SELECT). Synced records live in resources(resource_type, id, data); filter by resource_type and use json_extract on data, e.g. SELECT json_extract(data,'$.name') FROM resources WHERE resource_type='items'.
